name: United Airlines
type: US legacy carrier, Star Alliance anchor, NYSE:UAL
known_decisions: 2023: Added 40 new transatlantic routes without adding hub infrastructure — accepted lower load factors to crowd out Norse Atlantic
2022: Ordered 100 Boeing 787-9/10 Dreamliners specifically to replace ageing 767 fleet on transatlantic
2021: Extended Lufthansa JV to cover more O&D pairs under DOT anti-trust immunity
2020: Refused government bailout conditions that would have required slot divestitures at Newark
2019: Acquired gate positions at LHR by acquiring slots from rival carriers — paid ~$80M per slot pair
2018: Launched Polaris business class to close product gap with Delta One and BA Club Suite
financial_position: FY2024: Revenue $57.1B, EBITDA margin ~15%, net debt $17.5B. TATL RASM $0.1640 — highest in United's history. CapEx guide $3.8B/yr through 2026 focused on fleet renewal.
public_stance: CEO Scott Kirby has publicly committed to 'winning the premium transatlantic customer' — stated goal is closing RASM gap with Delta on business cabin by 2026. Explicitly stated United will defend every Newark slot 'to the last lawyer'.
demographic_prior: US mega-carrier incumbents protect hubs obsessively and use scale advantages (Star Alliance depth, corporate contracts) before price-competing. They are slow to reduce prices but fast to match competitor capacity additions. Loss aversion around slot holdings is structurally embedded — losing a slot is treated as permanent, irreversible damage.

game_theory_profile.decision_model: prospect theory with reference point anchoring on current slot portfolio
game_theory_profile.primary_bias: loss aversion — treats slot losses as 2.5x more painful than equivalent slot gains are beneficial
game_theory_profile.strategy_tendency: defend first, expand second — will absorb margin compression to protect slot base; expands opportunistically when competitors show financial weakness
known_weaknesses: Slow to adapt yield strategy to new entrants (Norse, Play) — took 3 quarters to respond with low-fare product
Over-indexed on corporate travel demand — vulnerable when WFH reduces business travel volumes
Newark slot concentration means a single regulatory decision can destabilise entire TATL network
Star Alliance coordination creates decision lag — must consult Lufthansa before solo capacity moves
game_theory_profile.rationality_derived: semi-rational — loss averse on slots, deliberate, Star Alliance constrained
